---
title: "Step 13: Gather Data With Ongoing Attention to Use"
description: "Detailed study notes for Step 13: Gather Data With Ongoing Attention to Use in Essentials of Utilization-Focused Evaluation."
book_id: "essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Step"
step_number: 13
source_pages: "349-360"
citation_key: "patton_2012_essentials_utilization_focused_evaluation"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation:step13-step-13-gather-data-with-ongoing-attention-to-use:c0001"
  - "chunk:essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation:step13-step-13-gather-data-with-ongoing-attention-to-use:c0012"
  - "chunk:essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation:step13-step-13-gather-data-with-ongoing-attention-to-use:c0023"
  - "chunk:essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation:utilization-focused-evaluation-checklist-17-steps-to-evaluations-that-are-useful:c0020"
source_status: "needs_review"
---

## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>2012</span><span>MCP evaluation-texts</span><span>Status: needs_review</span><span>Step 13</span><span>Source pages 349-360</span></div>

This note summarises step 13, "Gather Data With Ongoing Attention to Use," from Michael Quinn Patton's *Essentials of Utilization-Focused Evaluation* [@patton_2012_essentials_utilization_focused_evaluation]. It is a paraphrased study note based on source-linked MCP research-library retrieval records, not a substitute for the book section.

The retrieval trail is retained in the source records at the bottom of the page. The note paraphrases the section's argument and procedures, and it does not reproduce textbook tables, checklists, exhibits, graphics, or extended passages. When a checklist, exhibit, or graphic is central, the note describes its function and leaves the source artifact in the evidence record.

## Core Argument

Data collection should keep users informed and preserve the relationship between fieldwork decisions and intended use.

## Study Summary

Step 13 treats data gathering as part of utilization-focused facilitation, not a technical pause in stakeholder engagement. Primary intended users should remain appropriately informed as data collection unfolds.

The chapter emphasizes managing fieldwork with use in mind. Changes in access, measures, sample, schedule, or context should be interpreted for their implications for credibility and utility.

Ongoing attention to use does not mean users control every technical detail. It means the evaluator communicates consequential changes and keeps design decisions connected to the uses already negotiated.

This step prepares for Step 14 because data that are collected without attention to users' questions, comprehension, and ownership will be harder to organize for interpretation and action.

For close reading, track how this section uses intended use, credibility, primary intended users, and adaptive use while moving through data collection management, user communication, and fieldwork adaptation. The notes below identify what the section asks the evaluator to notice, what use problem it helps solve, and what should be checked against the saved evidence records before relying on the summary in applied work.

Within the wider book, this step belongs to the methods-and-data sequence: it negotiates credible evidence, tests use before data collection, and keeps fieldwork connected to utility.

## Key Concepts

- **intended use**: The specific way evaluation process or findings are expected to inform decisions, learning, action, or influence.
- **credibility**: The degree to which intended users judge evaluation evidence, methods, and interpretation as trustworthy enough for their uses.
- **primary intended users**: Named people expected to use the evaluation and participate in major focus, design, interpretation, and use decisions.
- **adaptive use**: Ongoing adjustment of evaluation questions, methods, communication, or follow-up as users, evidence, and context change.
- **evaluation facilitation**: The evaluator role of helping intended users clarify, negotiate, interpret, and act throughout the evaluation.

## Section Structure

- Data collection management.
- User communication.
- Fieldwork adaptation.
- Credibility monitoring.
- Handoff to interpretation.

## Practical Implications

- Keep users informed about consequential data collection developments.
- Track how fieldwork adaptations affect intended use.
- Prepare interpretation while data are being gathered, not only afterward.

## Concept Map

::: {.concept-map}
```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  Problem["Problem: What use problem does Step 13 help solve?"]
  Central["Concept: Gather Data With Ongoing Attention to Use"]
  Concept1["Concept: intended use"]
  Concept2["Concept: credibility"]
  Concept3["Concept: primary intended users"]
  Mechanism1["Mechanism: data collection management"]
  Mechanism2["Mechanism: user communication"]
  Mechanism3["Mechanism: fieldwork adaptation"]
  Implication1["Practice implication: Keep users informed about consequential data collection developments."]
  Implication2["Practice implication: Track how fieldwork adaptations affect intended use."]
  Question["Open question: What would make this usable in a live evaluation?"]

  Problem --> Central
  Central --> Question
  Central --> Concept1
  Central --> Concept2
  Central --> Concept3
  Concept1 --> Mechanism1
  Concept2 --> Mechanism2
  Concept3 --> Mechanism3
  Mechanism1 --> Implication1
  Mechanism2 --> Implication2
  Implication1 --> Question

  classDef problem fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#b91c1c,color:#7f1d1d;
  classDef concept fill:#e6f3f1,stroke:#115e59,color:#134e4a;
  classDef mechanism fill:#e8f1ff,stroke:#1d4ed8,color:#1e3a8a;
  classDef implication fill:#fce7f3,stroke:#be185d,color:#831843;
  class Problem problem;
  class Central concept;
  class Concept1,Concept2,Concept3 concept;
  class Mechanism1,Mechanism2,Mechanism3 mechanism;
  class Implication1,Implication2,Question implication;
```
:::

## Connections

- Book overview: [Overview](index.qmd)
- Reading route: [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)
- Concepts synthesis: [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)
- Practice synthesis: [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)
- Previous: [Step 12: Simulate Use of Findings: Evaluation's Equivalent of a Dress Rehearsal](step-12-simulate-use-of-findings.qmd)
- Next: [Step 14: Organize and Present the Data for Interpretation and Use by Primary Intended Users: Analysis, Interpretation, Judgment, and Recommendations](step-14-organize-and-present-data-for-interpretation-and-use.qmd)

## Study Prompts

1. What use problem does step 13 help solve?
2. Which primary intended users would need to participate for this guidance to work?
3. How would this section change scoping, methods, interpretation, reporting, or follow-up?
4. Where could this guidance be misused if treated as a fixed template?

## Source Records

<details class="source-chunks">
<summary>Source records</summary>
<ul class="source-chunk-list">
<li><code>chunk:essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation:step13-step-13-gather-data-with-ongoing-attention-to-use:c0001</code></li>
<li><code>chunk:essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation:step13-step-13-gather-data-with-ongoing-attention-to-use:c0012</code></li>
<li><code>chunk:essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation:step13-step-13-gather-data-with-ongoing-attention-to-use:c0023</code></li>
<li><code>chunk:essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation:utilization-focused-evaluation-checklist-17-steps-to-evaluations-that-are-useful:c0020</code></li>
</ul>
</details>

## References

- Patton, M. Q. (2012). *Essentials of Utilization-Focused Evaluation*. SAGE Publications. Step 13: Gather Data With Ongoing Attention to Use, pages 349-360.
- Saved retrieval metadata: `data/research-library/evidence/essentials-utilization-focused-evaluation/step-evidence-summary.json`.
